data.content:
The build confirms the issue. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**. SDK-style projects expand globs at evaluation time (before targets run), so the file generated during execution is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` after creating it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. SDK-style projects use implicit globbing at evaluation time, so the file generated during build execution isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, after generating it. This is the classic "including generated files" pattern.

Also, for proper clean support, the file should be added to `FileWrites`.

Let me fix this.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and track it for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's the diagnosis:

**Root cause:** SDK-style projects expand file globs at **evaluation time** (before any targets run). The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates the `.cs` file during **execution time**, so it's never picked up by the implicit `<Compile>` glob.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target, after the file is generated. This explicitly tells the compiler about the new file, and `FileWrites` ensures `dotnet clean` removes it.
